market_data.note: Market significantly stronger on Croatia than Elo model. Blended estimate used for downstream calibration.

rules_fired.RULE7_note: Panama 20.3% (model) / 14.3% (market). Model above 15% threshold but market at/below. RULE7 partially in effect for player props.
rules_fired.RULE8_note: Draw 28.65% > 25% threshold. BUT: survival match — both teams must win (JOR-ALG caveat). Apply cautiously; do NOT compress attacking props aggressively.
rules_fired.RULE15_note: Fajardo is expected-losing-team forward. Market says Panama 14.3% win (RULE7-adjacent). Full RULE15 suppression applied to Q9.
rules_fired.RULE_FOULS_note: Croatia slight possession/control favorite. Panama expected to defend more → Panama fouls more. But Croatia historical 13.0/game is high.
rules_fired.FLOOR_0p25_note: Applied to Q2 (offsides), Q7 (corners), Q9 (Fajardo SOT), Q10 (Sučić SOT).
rules_fired.RULE1_note: Market heavily weighted (-220 Croatia). 50% market / 50% model blended for match-outcome-driven props.

croatia_gd1.notes: Both Croatia goals in 1H. England scored twice in 2H. Croatia chased from 60' onward. Historical avg fouls: 13.0/game. Corner average WC: under 6.5 in 8 consecutive WC matches.

panama_gd1.notes: Dominant possession (62%) but 0 goals. 4 SOT vs Ghana compact block. 29 tackles — most by any team in WC2026 R1. Blackman (70') + Harvey (90') both received yellows — BOTH ON WARNINGS for GD2. Fajardo came on at 62', 0 SOT in 28 min.

jose_fajardo.rule15_note: Panama expected loser. Market 14.3% win = RULE7-adjacent. Full RULE15 suppression.
jose_fajardo.main_book_presence: not found on FanDuel/DraftKings scorer markets — signals low market confidence

luka_sucic.identity_warning: CRITICAL: Luka Sučić (Real Sociedad) ≠ Petar Sučić (Inter Milan). Petar STARTED vs England. Luka was UNUSED. No GD1 tournament data for Luka.
luka_sucic.rule15_note: Croatia expected WINNER. RULE15 does NOT apply to Croatia players.
luka_sucic.note: Dalić may switch to 4-3-3 for Panama match with Luka as key attacking option given fresh legs.

petar_sucic.note: Petar is different player from Q10 subject Luka. Petar set up Baturina goal vs England.

referee_profile.name: Pierre Atcho
referee_profile.note: High card-giver. AFCON-level physical style management. Warns teams early. With both Panama players on YC warnings and physical survival-match style expected, high card probability overall.

corners.note: GD1 evidence (2 corners each) conflicts with market. GD1 dominant per hierarchy. Generous λ=7 gives 27%. Croatia 'under 6.5 in 8 WC matches' aligns with GD1.

panama_offsides.note: Panama only 1 offside despite 62% possession vs Ghana. Not a pace-behind-lines team.

Q1.question: Will Panama commit more fouls than Croatia?
key_drivers: RULE_FOULS: Panama expected to defend more → Panama fouls more
BUT Croatia historical 13.0 fouls/game is very high
Panama GD1: 11 fouls while ATTACKING + 29 tackles
In this match: Panama defends → could foul 12-15; Croatia controls → might foul 9-12
Near coin-flip; slight YES lean for Panama based on defending-team principle

Q2.question: Will Panama be caught offside 2 or more times?
key_drivers: Panama GD1: only 1 offside despite 62% possession (not a pace-behind-lines team)
Poisson λ=1: P(≥2) = 0.264
Survival match: Panama will counter-attack more than GD1 → slight upward bump
Croatia defends with disciplined line; Panama lacks high-pace forwards
FLOOR_0.25 does not bind (0.32 > 0.25)

Q3.question: Will Panama have more shots on target than Croatia in the second half?
key_drivers: Market: Croatia -220 (68.75% win) vs model 51.0%. Market implies Croatia much stronger.
Blended Croatia win 59.9% → Croatia expected to dominate 2H
Croatia 2H SOT likely higher given quality edge (even in GD1 had shots; only lost because England 4-2 better)
Panama will push forward in 2H but Croatia will control pace and create more chances
Skellam P(PAN 2H SOT > CRO 2H SOT) in competitive match with Croatia quality: ~30%

Q4.question: Will Croatia score the first goal of the second half?
key_drivers: Model: 40.84% (P(Croatia scores first of 2H) = 60.22% × P(any 2H goal) 67.81%)
Survival match: both teams attack → higher total goal rate → adjust up slightly
Croatia quality edge means they're more likely to score in 2H than Panama
Croatia GD1: 0 goals in 2H, but vs England. vs Panama should be easier.
Market-calibrated: P(any 2H goal) adjusted to ~72% given match intensity

Q5.question: Will Croatia receive more cards than Panama?
key_drivers: Panama: Blackman + Harvey BOTH on yellow card warnings from GD1
Both will play (Panama needs all players for survival match)
Referee Atcho: 4.5+ YC/game at AFCON — high card-giver
Panama's physical style (29 tackles, 11 fouls while attacking GD1) = more cards when defending
Croatia: fresh card slate in GD2; likely fewer cards as possession team

Q6.question: Will Panama score at least 1 goal?
key_drivers: Model: P(PAN scores) = 59.42%
Market BTTS Yes -115 (53.5% implied) → P(PAN scores) ≈ 55-60% given P(CRO scores) ≈ 85%
Survival match: Panama MUST WIN → attacks aggressively regardless of score
Panama GD1: 4 SOT, 12 shots vs Ghana compact block with 62% possession
Both Blackman + Harvey on warnings but will still play aggressively on offense too

Q7.question: Will there be 9 or more total corner kicks?
key_drivers: Croatia GD1: only 2 corners vs England. Historical: under 6.5 corners in 8 consecutive WC matches.
Panama GD1: only 2 corners despite 62% possession (not a cross/corner-generating team)
Poisson with generous λ_total=7: P(≥9) = 27.1%; conservative λ=6: P(≥9) = 15.3%
Market Under 9.5 at -150 (60% implied for under; 40% for ≥10 corners)
GD1 evidence hierarchy: direct GD1 behavioral data > market corner lines
FLOOR_0.25 applies — estimate at 0.30 is above floor
Q7.edge: STRONGEST NO — biggest edge in match

Q8.question: Will Panama have 3 or more shots on target?
key_drivers: Panama GD1: 4 SOT vs Ghana (62% possession, compact Ghana block)
vs Croatia (1973 Elo, better defense than Ghana 1625): quality discount
Model 3.0× multiplier gives P(≥3 SOT|λ=0.9×3=2.7) = 52%
Adjusted for Croatian defensive quality: λ_SOT ≈ 2.5 → P(≥3) ≈ 46%
Survival match urgency pushes up slightly; near coin-flip

Q9.question: Will José Fajardo have at least 1 shot on target?
key_drivers: RULE15 FIRES FULLY: Panama market 14.3% win = RULE7-adjacent. Expected-losing-team forward.
GD1: 0 SOT in 28 min as substitute (came on 62' with Panama losing to Ghana)
Club rate 0.78 SOT/90 (Ecuador league — lower quality than WC opposition)
Likely to start given must-win (vs starting 28 min sub in GD1) but Croatian defense far better than Ghana
GHA-PAN lesson: crowd priced Fajardo at 0.57, correct was ~0.25-0.30 (RULE15 applied)
Combined probability with starting probability weighted: ~0.23 → FLOOR_0.25 → ESTIMATE: 0.28
Not found on FanDuel/DraftKings scorer markets = MAIN_BOOK_ABSENCE signal (<35%)

Q10.question: Will Luka Sučić have at least 1 shot on target?
key_drivers: IDENTITY CRITICAL: Luka Sučić (Real Sociedad) was UNUSED SUBSTITUTE vs England. Zero GD1 minutes.
Petar Sučić (Inter Milan, different player) started and played 90 min vs England.
Luka's starting status for GD2 uncertain: P(starts)=42%, P(sub)=30%, P(unused)=28%
Club rate 0.78 SOT/90 at Real Sociedad (La Liga — quality rate)
RULE15 does NOT apply: Croatia is the expected winner
Dalić may use Luka in 4-3-3 switch as fresh attacking option
Weighted calculation: 0.42×0.54 + 0.30×0.23 + 0.28×0 = 0.295 → estimate 0.30
CROWD RISK: crowd may conflate Luka with Petar (who started) and price much higher (~0.45)
Q10.edge: significant NO (amplified by Luka/Petar identity confusion)

edge_ranking.note: 9+ corners: GD1 (2 each), Croatia WC history, Panama possession-not-corners team. Crowd ~0.43.
Panama more 2H SOT: market says Croatia 69% win dominant. Crowd ~0.40.
Fajardo RULE15 full. GD1 0 SOT as sub. Market RULE7-adjacent. Crowd ~0.43.
Luka Sučić unused vs England. Crowd may conflate with Petar. Crowd ~0.43.
Panama more cards: physical style, two on warnings. Crowd ~0.47.
Panama scores: survival match urgency + GD1 capability. Crowd ~0.50.
Croatia first 2H goal: model 40.8%, calibrated up. Crowd ~0.38.
Panama offsides: 1 in GD1; survival match adds some. Crowd ~0.40.
Panama 3+ SOT: GD1 4 SOT but vs Ghana; Croatia better defense. Coin-flip.
Fouls: true coin-flip. Panama defends (fouls) vs Croatia historical 13.0/game.

Q1.question: Panama 2+ offsides
Q1.note: Panama 0 offsides. GD1 evidence (1 offside despite 62% possession) correctly predicted low offside count. Crowd at 44% overpriced counter-attack offside risk.

Q2.question: Panama more 2H SOT than Croatia
Q2.note: Panama did not have more 2H SOT than Croatia. Tiny beat — crowd was also close.

Q3.question: Croatia first goal of 2H
Q3.note: Croatia scored in 2H (the only goal, 1-0 result). Crowd at 54% was above us — they correctly read this more bullishly. Our 42% was too conservative.

Q4.question: Panama more fouls than Croatia
Q4.note: Panama committed more fouls. Crowd at 57% was above us — correctly read Panama would defend and foul. Our coin-flip (50%) was too neutral; RULE_FOULS should have pushed us to 0.55.

Q5.question: Croatia more cards than Panama
Q5.note: Croatia did not receive more cards than Panama. Tiny loss — very close to crowd. Cards likely went to Panama's physical players (Blackman, Harvey on warnings?).

Q6.question: Panama scores ≥1 goal
Q6.note: Panama did not score. Croatia 1-0. We overpriced Panama's scoring chance at 0.55 (model 59.4%). The market's Croatian dominance signal was correct.

Q7.question: 9+ total corners
Q7.note: 9+ corners YES — BIGGEST LOSS. GD1 evidence (2 corners each) was completely wrong for a survival match. The survival match attacking intensity (both teams desperate) massively inflated corners. Crowd at 51% was reading the market-implied probability correctly. The Under 9.5 -150 market line INCLUDED meaningful P(exactly 9) so P(≥9) was actually ~50-53%, not 27-30% as our Poisson suggested. CRITICAL CALIBRATION FAILURE: GD1 corners are unreliable when match-script changes from normal to both-teams-must-win.

Q8.question: Panama 3+ SOT
Q8.note: Panama <3 SOT. Croatian defense held. Our slight NO bias vs crowd was rewarded.

Q9.question: Fajardo ≥1 SOT
Q9.note: Fajardo 0 SOT. RULE15 confirmed again. Crowd at 40% still overpriced given RULE15 + Ecuador league level + Croatian defense quality.

Q10.question: Luka Sučić ≥1 SOT
Q10.note: Luka Sučić 0 SOT. Either unused again or played and didn't register SOT. Crowd at 37% may have conflated with Petar Sučić (who started GD1). Our Luka-specific identification and uncertain-start weighting paid off.

key_learnings.L1_CORNER_CATASTROPHE: Q7 -21.77: 9+ corners YES despite GD1 showing only 2 each. SURVIVAL MATCH CORNERS rule: When both teams are in must-win situations, corner production from GD1 is unreliable. The attacking intensity of survival matches creates end-to-end play with many dead balls. For survival matches, weight the market corner line at 80%+ rather than anchoring on GD1. P(≥9 corners) in a both-teams-must-win match should be treated as ~50% (crowd level) unless there is overwhelming structural reason to deviate.
key_learnings.L2_RULE_FOULS_Q4: Q4 -4.7: Panama more fouls YES. Our coin-flip (0.50) was too neutral. RULE_FOULS clearly said Panama defends → Panama fouls more. We should have been 0.55-0.58. The hesitation was Croatia's historical 13.0/game average, but in THIS match with Croatia having possession, Panama was the defending team. RULE_FOULS direction signal should have outweighed the magnitude uncertainty.
key_learnings.L3_Q3_CROATIA_FIRST_2H: Q3 -9.8: Croatia first 2H goal YES. Our 0.42 was too conservative; crowd at 0.54 was right. The sole goal of the match came in the 2H by Croatia. In a tight competitive match where Croatia controlled, they were more likely to nick a goal in 2H than our model suggested. Consider anchoring closer to crowd (0.50) when the match is competitive and Croatia is the slight favorite.
key_learnings.L4_RULE15_CONFIRMED_AGAIN: Q9 +10.62: Fajardo 0 SOT. RULE15 fully confirmed. Pattern: n=10+ now. Crowd consistently overprices expected-losing-team forwards. Always apply RULE15 in these situations.
key_learnings.L5_SUCIC_IDENTITY: Q10 +9.53: Luka Sučić 0 SOT. The identity distinction (Luka vs Petar) paid off. Crowd likely still partially confusing the two players and/or over-weighting starting probability.
